title: Used Audi A4 (B9) Buying Guide: Risks & What to Check | AutoVerdict
description: Buying a used Audi A4 B9? What to verify first — S tronic gearbox service, TFSI oil consumption, electronics, and the right questions for the seller.
intro: The B9 A4 is a refined, well-equipped used saloon and estate, but premium electronics and gearboxes can be costly out of warranty. Here is what to check before you view one.

faq.q: Is the Audi A4 B9 a reliable used car?
faq.a: The B9 A4 (2015–2023) is generally well regarded, with engines and gearboxes refined over earlier generations. The main ownership cost risk is expensive electronics and dual-clutch gearbox work later in life, so service history and a thorough electronics check matter.

faq.q: Does the Audi A4 have oil consumption problems?
faq.a: Higher oil consumption was more associated with some earlier 2.0 TFSI engines than the B9 generation. Even so, it is worth asking the owner about oil top-ups and checking the level and condition yourself on any used A4.

Quattro all-wheel drive adds traction and is popular in regions with winter weather, but adds a little complexity and running cost. For mostly dry, urban use front-wheel drive is simpler. Either way, confirm the relevant servicing was kept up.
